SOAP.Subjective: Patient reports achy and sharp pain on the outside of right elbow for several months, worsening recently. Pain is more noticeable at end of work, especially with wrist movements. Pain level is about a 4/10. No other joint or muscle pain, no numbness, tingling, coldness, or changes to senses. No fevers, chills, fatigue, cough, shortness of breath, chest pain, heart palpitations, dizziness, nausea, vomiting, urinary symptoms, memory loss, confusion, rashes, or skin changes. Patient is right-handed and the pain is causing issues with job.
SOAP.Objective: Patient denies any trauma or triggers to the area. Pain is alleviated by rest and over-the-counter NSAIDs (ibuprofen). No redness, swelling, misalignment, or deformities observed. Pain worsens with wrist movements, especially bending down. No pain reported when palpating medial aspect of elbow, forearm, or biceps.
SOAP.Assessment: Possible lateral epicondylitis (tennis elbow) due to repetitive strain at work
SOAP.Plan: Activity modifications at work to avoid repetitive wrist movements. Compression sleeve for support. Continue taking over-the-counter NSAIDs for pain relief. Physical therapy for mobility and strength in the arm. Ice application for pain and swelling if needed. Prognosis is 6 months to 2 years, with resolution typically occurring within a couple of months with physical therapy and compression/splints.
